body.title: Promote inherit-base-class to first-class palace rule
body.result: Promoted to first-class palace rule. Added theigors/rules/inherit-base-class (narrative covering: basemost class IS the logging+introspection layer for both Igor and agent_datacenter; lineage AgentBase → IgorBase; exemptions for Pydantic/Enum/ABC/Protocol/dataclass; existing D125 enforcement reference). Added theigors/rules/ticket_design_checks/inherit-base-class (check shape — applies_when/check_body/failure_message). Updated parent index to list the new check. Repo echo synced via palace_sync.py. No retrofitting (separate concern). Tests: 3988 passed.
body.description:
The base class IS the logging+introspection layer for both Igor and agent_datacenter. Every non-library class inherits from it; this is the foundational rule, currently informal.

Add palace rule at theigors/rules/inherit-base-class with the narrative (every class inherits → logging is as easy as print → introspection comes for free). Add corresponding check shape at theigors/rules/ticket_design_checks/inherit-base-class — fires when ticket adds new classes, requires the ticket to declare base-class inheritance.

This ticket does NOT retrofit existing violations. A separate ticket audits the codebase for non-inheriting classes.

**Affected files:** new palace nodes theigors/rules/inherit-base-class and theigors/rules/ticket_design_checks/inherit-base-class. No code changes.
**Design rules:** no persistence beyond palace. no-new-memory-schemas: reusing memory_palace.
**Test plan:** rule appears in palace dump; audit-ticket includes it in /review filing-time checks; a draft ticket adding a class without inheritance gets AMEND.
